description: Add test coverage for untested payment module
system_context: SaaS application with a payment module that has 0% test coverage. Stack: TypeScript, Express, Stripe SDK, PostgreSQL. The payment module handles subscription creation, webhook processing, and invoice management. It was written 6 months ago and never tested. Coverage requirement: reach 80% before the next release.
turns: Starting test coverage for the payment module. Running coverage report: src/payments/ has 0% coverage across 8 files: payment.service.ts (234 lines), webhook.handler.ts (189 lines), subscription.service.ts (156 lines), invoice.service.ts (98 lines), stripe.client.ts (67 lines), payment.controller.ts (123 lines), subscription.controller.ts (89 lines), invoice.controller.ts (56 lines).

Read src/payments/stripe.client.ts. It wraps the Stripe SDK. Exports a singleton stripeClient. The key insight is that all payment operations go through this client. For tests, I need to mock this module. Will use jest.mock('../stripe.client') pattern.

Read src/payments/payment.service.ts. It has 6 public methods: createPaymentIntent, confirmPayment, refundPayment, getPaymentHistory, createCustomer, attachPaymentMethod. The createPaymentIntent calls stripe.paymentIntents.create. Most methods do a Stripe API call then write to the database via Prisma.

Created src/payments/__tests__/payment.service.test.ts. Mocked both stripeClient and PrismaClient. Writing tests for createPaymentIntent: happy path creates intent and saves to DB, handles Stripe errors by throwing PaymentError, validates amount is positive integer.

Added tests for confirmPayment (4 tests), refundPayment (3 tests), getPaymentHistory (2 tests), createCustomer (3 tests), attachPaymentMethod (2 tests). Total: 19 tests for payment.service.ts. All passing.

Read src/payments/webhook.handler.ts. This is the complex one. It handles 8 different Stripe webhook event types: payment_intent.succeeded, payment_intent.payment_failed, customer.subscription.created, customer.subscription.deleted, customer.subscription.updated, invoice.paid, invoice.payment_failed, charge.refunded. Each has different logic.

Created src/payments/__tests__/webhook.handler.test.ts. The key challenge is constructing valid Stripe webhook event objects for each type. Used Stripe's constructEvent mock. Writing tests for each event type.

Added 8 tests for webhook event handling, one per event type. The payment_intent.succeeded test was tricky - it updates the payment record in DB and sends a confirmation email via the notification service. Had to mock notificationService.sendPaymentConfirmation. All 8 passing.

Read src/payments/subscription.service.ts. Has 5 methods: createSubscription, cancelSubscription, upgradeSubscription, downgradeSubscription, getSubscriptionStatus. Complex business logic in upgrade/downgrade involving proration calculations.

Created src/payments/__tests__/subscription.service.test.ts. 15 tests covering all 5 methods including edge cases: cancel at period end vs immediately, upgrade mid-cycle proration, downgrade pending next cycle. All 15 passing.

Read src/payments/invoice.service.ts. 3 methods: getInvoiceList, getInvoiceById, downloadInvoicePDF. The downloadInvoicePDF calls Stripe's invoice.retrieve and then fetches the hosted_invoice_url. Created 6 tests. All passing.

Running full coverage report for src/payments/: payment.service.ts 94%, webhook.handler.ts 88%, subscription.service.ts 91%, invoice.service.ts 100%, stripe.client.ts 72%, payment.controller.ts 78%, subscription.controller.ts 82%, invoice.controller.ts 89%. Overall: 87% coverage. Target was 80%. Exceeded.
Final count: 48 new tests added. All 48 passing. Coverage went from 0% to 87%. Committed: test: add payment module test coverage (87%, 48 tests).
